back: DNS translates a domain name into the IP address of the server.

mcq.question: What does DNS do?
mcq.correct: Translates domain names into IP addresses

back: An IP address identifies a device on a network so data can be sent to the correct destination.

mcq.question: What is the main purpose of an IP address?
mcq.correct: To identify a device on a network

back: A router forwards packets between different networks and chooses a path for the data.

mcq.question: Which device forwards packets between networks?

back: Data is broken into packets, sent across the network, and reassembled at the destination.

mcq.question: What happens in packet switching?
mcq.correct: Data is broken into packets and reassembled later
wrong: The whole file travels as one unbroken unit
Only wired networks can be used
Packets are stored permanently in RAM
